name: Glassmorphism UI Patterns
description: Premium glassmorphism design patterns for Antigravity IDX apps.

glass-container.description: Standard glass container with backdrop blur and reflective overlay.
glass-container.css: .glass-card { background: rgba(20, 20, 36, 0.4); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08); position: relative; overflow: hidden; } .glass-card::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1) 0%, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0) 100%); pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.5; }

background-blobs.css: .bg-blob { position: absolute; width: 500px; height: 500px; filter: blur(140px); opacity: 0.1; border-radius: 50%; z-index: -1; animation: blob-float 20s infinite alternate; }

sdlc-phase-context.description: Context-aware UI logic for mapping routes to SDLC phases.
sdlc-phase-context.logic: const getPhase = (path) => { if (path === '/') return 'Ideation'; if (path.startsWith('/workflow')) return 'Architecture'; ... }

best_practices: Use subtle borders (rgba) instead of solid colors.
Ensure backdrop-filter has a -webkit prefix for Safari support.
Limit blur radius to 16-20px for performance on low-end devices.
Always use a dark base background to make glass effects pop.
anti_patterns: High opacity on backgrounds (breaks the glass effect).
Overlapping too many glass layers (causes blur artifacts).
Pure white text on bright glass (use subtle shadows or lower brightness).
